definition.name: face_dimension
definition.definition: For a convex set K in a (possibly infinite-dimensional) topological vector space and a face F of K, the dimension of F, denoted dim(F), is the dimension of the affine hull of F (the smallest affine subspace containing F). Equivalently, dim(F) = dim(aff(F)) where aff(F) = {Σ λ_i x_i : x_i ∈ F, Σ λ_i = 1, finite sums}. For a point x ∈ K, dim(F_K(x)) = 0 if and only if x is an extreme point of K. In the context of state spaces of *-algebras, the face-commutant correspondence gives dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1, relating geometric face dimension to algebraic commutant dimension. The "-1" arises from the normalisation constraint ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1 within the face.
